steps.title: Locate the main verb of the sentence
steps.content: The sentence already has a finite main verb later on: "Author Madeline L'Engle... begins her novel A Wrinkle in Time with descriptions of..." The blank sits inside a phrase set off by commas between the subject and that main verb.

steps.title: Choose a non-finite form for the modifier slot
steps.content: A modifier between subject and main verb must not introduce a second finite verb. A present participle (-ing) forms a participial phrase that modifies the subject: "L'Engle, looking to create a suspenseful tone..., begins..."

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'looked': finite past-tense verb; produces two main verbs (looked / begins) for one subject. Verb-form error. ✗
  • Choice B — 'looks': finite present-tense verb; same two-main-verb problem. Verb-form error. ✗
  • Choice C — 'is looking': finite progressive; still a second main verb attached to L'Engle. Verb-form error. ✗
  • Choice D — 'looking': present participle forming a modifier phrase, leaving "begins" as the lone main verb. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
